title: pentanema × litorale
intro:
Pentanema × litorale, also known as Littoral Fleabane, is a hybrid plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae), resulting from the cross between the species Pentanemopsis aristata and Pentanemopsis aristata subsp. aristata is not a valid parent taxon, Pentanemopsis aristata subsp. aristata is not a valid parent taxon but Pentanemopsis aristata is and Pentanema rubra . However, Pentanema × litorale is a hybrid of Pentanema rubra and Pentanema fruticosa  it is a hybrid of Pentanema rubra and Pentanema fruticosa. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions.

Pentanema × litorale has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and promoting wound healing.

The herbal preparations of Pentanema × litorale have been used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections, wounds, and digestive issues. The plant's medicinal properties have also been used to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and promote relaxation.
